edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The current DR Tulu 8B RL checkpoint was initialized from DR-Tulu-SFT-8B before RL training.

evidence.explanation: The release card explicitly states that DR Tulu 8B was trained on top of the SFT checkpoint, establishing immediate checkpoint initialization lineage.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The RL stage for DR Tulu 8B used the DR Tulu RL data mix. The public dataset card notes that the released dataset contains the OpenScholar and SearchArena subsets, while the final RL training additionally sampled RaR-Science and RaR-Medicine prompts.

evidence.excerpt: This model has undergone RL training on this dataset.
evidence.explanation: The subject model card directly states that the RL checkpoint was trained on the linked RL dataset.

evidence.excerpt: This does not contain the RaR datasets we use in final RL training
evidence.explanation: The RL dataset card clarifies that the public release is only part of the final RL mix and that RaR subsets were also used during the subject's RL training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: RL prompt curation drew long-form prompts from SearchArena. The paper reports roughly 5K new RL prompts jointly collected from SearchArena and OpenScholar, using prompts not used during SFT; the release dataset card also says the released RL data contains SearchArena prompts.

evidence.excerpt: collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena
evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly names SearchArena as a source of RL prompts for DR Tulu's RL stage.

evidence.excerpt: generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: The released RL dataset card confirms that SearchArena prompts are part of the RL training data pipeline.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: RL prompt curation also drew long-form prompts from OpenScholar queries. The paper reports roughly 5K new RL prompts jointly collected from OpenScholar and SearchArena, disjoint from the SFT prompt set.

evidence.explanation: The paper identifies OpenScholar as one of the two prompt sources for the RL stage.

evidence.excerpt: generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: The released RL dataset card confirms that OpenScholar prompts are part of the RL training data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The final RL mix additionally used the first 3,000 samples from RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini as long-form RL prompts with inherited initial rubrics.

evidence.explanation: The paper states that RaR prompts were part of the RL stage.

evidence.excerpt: We sample the first 3000 samples from RaR-Science
evidence.explanation: The RL dataset card gives the exact RaR-Science sample count used in final RL training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The final RL mix additionally used the first 1,000 samples from RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini as long-form RL prompts with inherited initial rubrics.

evidence.explanation: The paper states that RaR prompts were included in RL training.

evidence.excerpt: and the first 1000 samples from RaR-Medicine
evidence.explanation: The RL dataset card gives the exact RaR-Medicine sample count used in final RL training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini generated the initial search-based rubrics used in RL data construction for OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts; the same model family also generated rubric content during the RL loop.

evidence.excerpt: used GPT-4.1-mini as the rubric generation model
evidence.explanation: This directly states that GPT-4.1-mini generated the RL rubrics.
The training-details section says GPT-4.1-mini was used for generation-related judge roles during RL training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini was the in-loop RL judge for rubric scoring and citation scoring during DR Tulu 8B's RL training, so its judgments directly shaped rewards and therefore the final weights.

evidence.excerpt: rubric scoring, generation, and for citation scoring
evidence.explanation: The training details explicitly identify GPT-4.1-mini as the LM judge for reward-related scoring roles in RL.

evidence.excerpt: Our in-loop judge model uses GPT-4.1-mini
evidence.explanation: This confirms GPT-4.1-mini was the RL in-loop judge, i.e. a direct reward/filtering dependency.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: HealthBench was part of the released DR Tulu 8B evaluation suite for long-form deep-research performance.

evidence.explanation: The released model card lists HealthBench in the official evaluation table for this checkpoint.
The paper names HealthBench as one of the long-form benchmarks used to evaluate DR Tulu.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ResearchQA was part of the released DR Tulu 8B evaluation suite; the paper later notes that they used the official 776-question deep-research subset.

evidence.explanation: The model card explicitly lists ResearchQA among the reported evaluation benchmarks.
The paper names ResearchQA as an evaluation benchmark and later describes evaluating on its official 776-question subset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu 8B was evaluated on the ScholarQA-CSv2 / SQA-CS-V2 task in AstaBench, reported in the model card as SQAv2.

evidence.explanation: The subject model card reports SQAv2 scores for DR Tulu 8B.
The paper resolves the SQAv2 shorthand to the AstaBench ScholarQA-CSv2 benchmark.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DeepResearch Bench (DRB) was part of the subject's official long-form evaluation suite.

evidence.explanation: The model card explicitly reports DeepResearch Bench scores for the release.
The paper names DRB as one of the four main long-form benchmarks.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SimpleQA was part of DR Tulu 8B's short-form evaluation suite.

evidence.explanation: The model card reports SimpleQA results for the release.
The paper explicitly includes SimpleQA in the short-form evaluation suite.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: 2WikiMultiHopQA was part of DR Tulu 8B's short-form evaluation suite; the paper says they randomly selected 1,000 2Wiki questions for efficient evaluation.

evidence.explanation: The subject model card reports 2Wiki results.
The paper explicitly names 2Wiki in the short-form benchmark suite.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: WebWalkerQA was part of DR Tulu 8B's short-form evaluation suite.

evidence.explanation: The release card reports WebWalker scores.
The paper names WebWalkerQA as a short-form evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The release paper evaluates DR Tulu 8B on GeneticDiseasesQA, a new out-of-domain benchmark of 47 questions over 24 pathogenic gene variants that the team constructed and also released under rl-research.

evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly describes GeneticDiseasesQA as an evaluation dataset used for DR Tulu.
The rl-research organization page shows that the team released a dataset handle corresponding to GeneticDiseasesQA.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 served as an evaluation judge for the short-form suite, for GeneticDiseasesQA, and the paper notes that downstream HealthBench evaluation also uses GPT-4.1.

evidence.explanation: This shows GPT-4.1 judged the short-form benchmark suite.

evidence.excerpt: used GPT-4.1 to score each response
evidence.explanation: This directly states that GPT-4.1 judged GeneticDiseasesQA responses.
The paper explicitly identifies GPT-4.1 as the HealthBench downstream judge model.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini was used as the ResearchQA evaluation judge on the official 776-question subset used for deep-research systems.

evidence.explanation: The paper directly states that GPT-4.1-mini judged ResearchQA.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Gemini 2.5 Flash was used as the evaluation judge for SQAv2 and for the FACT component of DeepResearch Bench.

evidence.explanation: The paper states that Gemini 2.5 Flash judged SQAv2.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini-2.5-Pro for RACE and Gemini-2.5-Flash for FACT
evidence.explanation: Because DR Tulu uses the official DRB code, this README identifies Gemini 2.5 Flash as the FACT judge used in that evaluation pipeline.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Through the official DeepResearch Bench evaluation code path used by DR Tulu, Gemini 2.5 Pro served as the RACE evaluator for article-quality scoring.

evidence.explanation: The DR Tulu paper says DRB evaluation was run with the benchmark's official code.
The official DRB repository states that Gemini 2.5 Pro is used for RACE, so it is an evaluation judge dependency for DR Tulu's DRB results.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The released DR-Tulu-No-RLER-8B checkpoint is the explicit no-RLER ablation used to analyze the effect of evolving rubrics versus fixed/search-based rubrics during RL training.

evidence.explanation: The release card explicitly identifies DR-Tulu-No-RLER-8B as an ablation artifact relative to the main DR-Tulu-8B release.
The paper's rubric ablation explains the experimental comparison that the no-RLER checkpoint represents.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The team ran a 'No SFT' cold-start ablation that began RL directly from Qwen3-8B instead of an SFT checkpoint to study the effect of SFT initialization on downstream RL performance.

evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly describes a no-SFT ablation that starts RL from Qwen3, making Qwen3-8B an ablation artifact.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: HealthBench was one of the benchmark axes used in the paper's SFT-mixture, cold-start, and rubric ablation analyses.

evidence.explanation: HealthBench appears as an analysis axis in the SFT-data ablation, RL-starting-point analysis, and rubric-ablation table.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ResearchQA was one of the benchmark axes used in the paper's SFT-mixture and rubric ablation analyses.

evidence.explanation: ResearchQA appears as an explicit benchmark column/axis in the paper's ablation figures and tables.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SQAv2 (the ScholarQA-CSv2 task in AstaBench) was used in the paper's cold-start and rubric ablation analyses.

evidence.explanation: SQAv2 is explicitly shown as an analysis benchmark in the ablation plots and rubric-ablation table.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DeepResearch Bench was used in the paper's SFT-mixture, cold-start, and rubric ablation analyses.

evidence.explanation: DRB is explicitly used as an analysis benchmark in the ablation figures and table.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu SFT 8B was initialized from Qwen3-8B before supervised fine-tuning for deep-research tool use.

evidence.explanation: The SFT model card explicitly states that the checkpoint was trained on top of Qwen3-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu SFT 8B was trained on the DR Tulu SFT data mix. The public release card notes that this published dataset omits some training subsets (MegaScience, HotpotQA, and ScholarQA-derived slices) even though they were part of the actual SFT mixture described in the paper.

evidence.excerpt: This model has undergone SFT training on this dataset.
evidence.explanation: The SFT model card directly identifies the linked SFT dataset as a training input.

evidence.excerpt: This does not contain the SFT subsets created using prompts from MegaScicen, HotpotQA, and ScholarQA.
evidence.explanation: The dataset card clarifies that the public release is an incomplete view of the full SFT mix used for the model.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: OpenScholar queries were a long-form prompt source for SFT; Table 8 reports 5,704 long-form OpenScholar-derived examples in the SFT mix.

evidence.excerpt: source prompts are curated from OpenScholar
evidence.explanation: The SFT dataset card directly lists OpenScholar as a prompt source.
The paper provides the exact number of OpenScholar-derived long-form SFT examples.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: SearchArena conversations were a long-form prompt source for SFT; Table 8 reports 3,547 long-form SearchArena-derived examples.

evidence.explanation: The SFT dataset card explicitly lists Search Arena among prompt sources.
The paper gives the exact number of Search Arena long-form SFT examples.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The SFT mix included 1,000 long-form examples derived from Ai2 ScholarQA traces. The team transformed non-iterative ScholarQA traces into iterative-search trajectories before using them for SFT.

evidence.excerpt: We used the trajectory data from Ai2 ScholarQA
evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly states that Ai2 ScholarQA trajectory data was used to create SFT data.
The paper gives the size of the ScholarQA-derived portion and explains that these traces were transformed into iterative-search data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: HotpotQA supplied short-form, verifiable QA prompts for SFT; Table 8 reports 1,176 HotpotQA examples.

evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly states that HotpotQA was sampled into the SFT mixture.
The paper gives the exact number of HotpotQA SFT examples.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: TaskCraft supplied short-form prompts for SFT; Table 8 reports 583 TaskCraft examples.

evidence.explanation: The SFT dataset card lists TaskCraft among short-form QA prompt sources.
The paper gives the exact TaskCraft count in the SFT data mix.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The SFT mix included the silver subset of WebWalkerQA ('WebWalker-Silver'); Table 8 reports 1,438 such short-form examples.

evidence.explanation: The SFT dataset card explicitly names WebWalker-Silver as a source.
The paper gives the exact count for the WebWalker-Silver portion of SFT data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: MegaScience supplied short-form science reasoning prompts for SFT; Table 8 reports 814 MegaScience examples.

evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly names MegaScience as part of SFT prompt curation.
The paper reports the exact number of MegaScience examples in the SFT mix.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: PopQA contributed to the short-form SFT mixture; the appendix reports a combined 874 examples for PopQA and TyDi QA, but does not break that count down by source.

evidence.explanation: The SFT dataset card lists PopQA among short-form prompt sources.
The paper confirms that PopQA contributed to a combined PopQA/TyDi QA SFT slice, though the split is not separately reported.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: TyDi QA (English) contributed to the short-form SFT mixture; the appendix reports a combined 874 examples for PopQA and TyDi QA, without a per-source breakdown.

evidence.explanation: The SFT dataset card lists TyDiQA (English) as a source of short-form prompts.
The paper confirms that TyDi QA contributed to the combined PopQA/TyDi QA SFT slice.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-5 acted as the long-form prompt-quality judge during SFT data curation, scoring OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts and filtering by threshold before trajectory generation.

evidence.explanation: This directly states that GPT-5 filtered long-form prompts during SFT data creation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-5 generated end-to-end SFT teacher trajectories, including reasoning traces, tool calls, and citation-backed answers; it also generated a few hundred interleaved search-and-think trajectories for BrowseComp-style questions.

evidence.explanation: The main method section says GPT-5 was prompted to produce entire end-to-end trajectories for SFT.
The appendix adds that GPT-5 also generated interleaved search-and-think trajectories for BrowseComp-style prompts.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 contributed two distinct SFT-data generation roles: it created 916 BrowseComp-style short-form questions, and it created section-level sub-queries that transformed Ai2 ScholarQA traces into iterative-search SFT trajectories.

evidence.explanation: This directly attributes the BrowseComp-style SFT questions to GPT-4.1.

evidence.excerpt: used GPT-4.1 to create a sub-query
evidence.explanation: This directly attributes the ScholarQA-trace transformation sub-queries to GPT-4.1.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The SFT recipe explicitly borrowed the BrowseComp task style: instead of training on the original benchmark directly, the team asked GPT-4.1 to synthesize 916 BrowseComp-style questions for the SFT mix.

evidence.explanation: The paper explicitly says the generated SFT questions were BrowseComp-style, making BrowseComp a methodological inspiration rather than a direct training leaf.
Table 8 shows that the SFT mix included a dedicated BrowseComp-style slice, confirming its role in shaping the recipe.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b
edges.description: Qwen3-8B is the post-trained variant built on top of Qwen3-8B-Base.

evidence.explanation: The official Qwen3-8B card identifies Qwen3-8B-Base as its base model, establishing immediate checkpoint lineage.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-no-rler-8b
edges.description: The released DR-Tulu-No-RLER-8B ablation checkpoint was initialized from DR-Tulu-SFT-8B before its RL run without RLER.

evidence.explanation: The ablation model card explicitly states that it was trained on top of the SFT checkpoint.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-no-rler-8b
edges.description: The released DR-Tulu-No-RLER-8B ablation checkpoint used the same DR Tulu RL dataset but was trained without RLER.

evidence.excerpt: undergone RL training on this dataset
evidence.explanation: The ablation model card identifies the RL dataset used for training.
This clarifies that the ablation shares the RL data pipeline while differing in the rubric-learning method.
